summary: Transforms a non-empty structured property string and associated data into a nested parsed object according to parsing options.
signature_intent: Accepts a property expression, data to assign, parser configuration, and parsed-value metadata; returns a nested object representation or no result when validation blocks parsing.
behavior: Returns no result when the property expression is empty or otherwise falsy.
Optionally treats dot notation as nested bracket notation when configured to do so.
Extracts the top-level property and nested bracket segments up to the configured depth limit.
Rejects properties that would target built-in object prototype fields unless prototype access is explicitly allowed or plain-object mode is enabled.
Preserves any remaining unprocessed nested path beyond the depth limit as a single trailing property segment.
Delegates final nested object construction to the object parser using the collected path segments and supplied data.
edge_cases: A top-level property matching a protected prototype field returns no result under default protection settings.
A nested property matching a protected prototype field returns no result under default protection settings.
When depth is zero or less, bracket parsing is skipped and the full property expression is treated as the top-level property.
When the depth limit is reached before the full expression is consumed, the remaining path is grouped into one final segment.
If there is no top-level property before the first bracketed segment, only nested segments are used.
forbidden_details: Original variable names, repository names, source paths, and incidental implementation quirks are intentionally omitted.
